sources.name: Devpost hackathon projects
sources.notes: Primary source for hackathon-style product seeds. Preserve award context, project URL, demo/repo links, input-output transformation, and anti-clone boundary.

sources.name: AI hackathon winner pages
sources.notes: Use only public result pages and primary hackathon/showcase pages. Do not treat sponsor platforms as source products.

sources.name: community, university, and company demo showcases
sources.notes: Good for non-obvious small ideas. Record uncertainty if award status is unclear.

sources.name: GitHub Trending
sources.notes: Prefer recent momentum and demoable behavior over total star count. Avoid already-canonical mega-products.

sources.name: GitHub Search
sources.notes: Use public metadata only. Preserve query, stars, forks, issues, pushed_at, topics, and why the repo is small/rising.

sources.name: Hacker News Show HN / Launch HN
sources.notes: Use story metadata and linked repo/product pages by default. Do not ingest long comment bodies unless explicitly approved.

sources.name: Product Hunt launches
sources.notes: Capture metadata, positioning, and audience reaction. Do not use already dominant products as source cards.

sources.name: Hugging Face Spaces
sources.notes: Strong source for small interactive AI demos. Preserve task, input, output, likes/usage signals when visible.

sources.name: Indie maker launches and product galleries
sources.notes: Use as qualitative market signal, not proof of market size.

sources.name: AI tool directories and public product galleries
sources.notes: Directory entries are weak unless they link to a live demo, product page, or clear public reaction.

sources.name: Google Trends / current events
sources.notes: Use only as remix target context. Do not let broad trends replace small product source cards.

sources.name: public civic and education context
sources.notes: Useful for target themes after a small product value core has been selected.
